scenario: Generic safe project-local validation script claim is detected without hardcoding one external script name
contextMode: synthetic scanner fixture plus local output-contract test
fixtureState: Synthetic Gradle-wrapper project with an executable safe project-local shell script; raw instruction prose and local paths are omitted.
taskSummary: Choose whether Habitat should recognize only a previously observed script name or any bounded safe project-local validation shell script mentioned in validation context.
relevantHabitatSignals: Repository facts select the executable Gradle wrapper.
Project guidance mentions a safe ./scripts/*.sh validation command.
Generated JSON records only the sanitized command, not raw instruction prose.
Generated context emits bounded uncertainty before using raw package-manager commands.
Generated context promotes the executable project-local script ahead of raw Gradle commands.

prefer: record the sanitized project-local script claim
prefer the executable project-local script before raw package-manager validation
keep selected package-manager guidance after the script entrypoint
askFirst: Git/GitHub mutation before publishing Habitat-side evidence
doNot: treat non-shell examples as validation claims
expand into broad Android environment auditing
reviewFirst: command_policy.md before staging, committing, or pushing the Habitat-side fixture

withPreviousBehavior.firstProposedAction: Follow raw Gradle wrapper guidance because only the hardcoded previously observed script name could become a validation claim.
withPreviousBehavior.risk: A repository-specific validation wrapper could be bypassed even when project docs make it the intended local check.

withHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Record the safe project-local script claim, verify whether it wraps Gradle validation, then prefer it before raw Gradle commands.
avoidedCommands: run raw ./gradlew before checking the documented validation wrapper

evidenceNormalizationImplication: The existing project-local ValidationCommandClaim boundary is sufficient; the improvement is a narrower command extractor, not a broad normalized-evidence layer.

verdict.reason: Habitat now recognizes safe ./scripts/*.sh validation claims, keeps unsafe or example-only script paths out, and still promotes only executable scripts into the short Prefer list.
verdict.followUpImprovement: Only broaden project-local script shapes if a real repository shows a command-changing validation wrapper outside ./scripts/*.sh.

artifactImprovementIfFailed: If agents still miss safe validation wrappers, tighten the bounded script extractor before adding ecosystem-specific scanning.
